refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain a packet loss rate of ≤ 1% for teleoperation control streams under high-latency network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original requirement was vague regarding 'customer-defined' limits; specifying the 1% threshold aligns with the provided system constraints and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Network stress testing using a packet analyzer during simulated teleoperation sessions with induced jitter and latency.

candidate_dps: Quality of Service (QoS) tagging and traffic shaping
selected_dp.selected_dp: Quality of Service (QoS) tagging and traffic shaping

potential_sources: Bandwidth contention with high-resolution video streams
coupling_check.notes: QoS logic must be synchronized between the vehicle and the teleoperation station to ensure end-to-end priority.

assumptions_risks: Assumes the underlying network hardware supports Layer 3 DSCP tagging.
Risk: Network Link provider may ignore QoS tags.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Integrate FEC as a secondary DP if QoS alone does not meet the 1% threshold.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Manages the remote control logic, operator interface, and the communication protocol stack for remote driving.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and data link layer for packet transmission.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Status of packet loss and link quality sent to the operator.

module_containment_scores.notes: Too broad; would diffuse the logic across the entire system.
Only handles the receiving end of the teleoperation stream.
Primary owner of the control loop and latency-sensitive data.
Safety services react to packet loss but do not manage the communication quality itself.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing QoS logic within the Teleoperation module minimizes interface complexity and ensures the control loop is directly aware of link quality.
containment_risks: Dependency on the 'Network Link' interface which is often managed by third-party providers.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define a specific 'Communication Manager' sub-component within the Teleoperation module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: QoS tagging is a standard and effective DP for meeting packet loss requirements in software-intensive systems.

diffusion_risks: Logic might leak into the Vehicle module if the vehicle needs to acknowledge priority packets.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module is the most logical home for managing the end-to-end control stream quality.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High; packet loss can be measured at the Teleoperation module boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation to the Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define the handshake between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules for QoS synchronization.

decision.justification: The FR-DP-IM mapping is sound, but the FR is not atomic and requires further decomposition into monitoring, tagging, and recovery sub-functions.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-009.1: Tag control packets with high-priority DSCP values.
FR-NGHE-009.2: Implement a heartbeat mechanism to detect link failure within 100ms.
FR-NGHE-009.3: Throttle non-essential telemetry during periods of high congestion.

open_risks: Hardware limitations on the Vehicle side for processing high-frequency QoS-tagged packets.
evidence_needed: Network provider specification for QoS support.
